**Remlin Ast'eria** 

(*Remlin of the Turning Sky; ‘Owl’ to close friends and tutors*)

*“The stars command all of the cosmos, but perhaps not us.”*

* Race: High Elf  
* Age: \~200 years  
* Alignment: Neutral Good  
* Class: Druid (eventual Subclass: [Circle of the Stars](https://www.dndbeyond.com/classes/2190878-druid#CircleoftheStars))  
* Background: [Sage](https://www.dndbeyond.com/backgrounds/406485-sage)  
* Religion: Oros al'Calhoon [\[The Twin Soul\]](https://www.worldanvil.com/w/abryryim-clarkjo03/a/age-of-steel3A-the-gods-article)

**Backstory**

Remlin was born in Abryryim on a night lit by an unusual alignment of stars.  His father, a scholar of lore, died the very day he was born, leaving him in the care of his mother, a devout high elf priestess of Oros.  Not long after, she heard the call of Oros and departed Abryryim for the Feywild to continue her service among the fey and the celestial, leaving young Remlin to grow under the trusted guidance of Druid elders she had spent decades with in Abryryim.

From his earliest years, Remlin showed a natural affinity for the Druidic arts, though he always sensed something else teasing at his attention. The elders recognized his aptitude to natural studies, teaching him the balance of life, the cycles of the seasons, and the hidden rhythms of the wild. By his teenage years, Remlin began receiving fragments of visions from the stars—fleeting, symbolic, and often mysterious. These visions pulled at him, whispering secrets he could not yet fully understand.  Not long after, Remlin left the Druidic enclaves for the mountain peaks, seeking the tutelage of *The Turning Sky* Circle of Star Druids and their Orcish Shaman allies. There, he found a home among those who studied the night sky, astrology, and the movements of the planes and their impact on Abryryim. For a decade, he honed his skills in astronomy, cosmology, planar teachings, and traditional druidic practices—hiking, fishing, hunting, and communing with the spirits of nature.

One night, while mapping the heavens alone upon a tall peak, Remlin believes that *Oros al'Calhoon* herself visited him in a vision. The Moon Goddess revealed a glimpse of a possible prophecy of the end times, a dark and sprawling vision that shook him to his core. Convinced that he had a part to play in understanding and perhaps preparing for what was to come, Remlin convinced the Druids and Shamans to use their Circle Casting to send him to the Feywild to study with the feyfolk and with his mother, gaining a deeper understanding of cosmos and their light as both guides and warnings.

During his decades in the Feywild, he was drawn by twilight visions while hunting to the recently-deceased corpse of an Owlbear deep in a sylvan grove.  In what felt like a trance, Remlin meticulously removed the hide over three days and three nights and spent the next tenday curing the hide and stitching leather \- sustaining himself only on cooked owlbear meat.  When he awoke from his Trance, Remlin noticed that he had begun etching the inside of the cured owlbear hide with constellations and images from his astrological visions.  He returned to his mother who, in time, helped him decipher what seemed to be fragments of a celestial map.  Each constellation—The Chalice Bearer, The Archer Unbound, The Star-Dragon, The Broken Crown, The Twin Paths, and the Extinguished Sky—represents guidance, prophecy, and choice.  Occasionally when Remlin is wearing the cloak and the stars are in alignment, he swears he can *feel* the constellations pulsing against him...symbols that almost seem to “speak” to him, guiding his visions.

After fifty years away, Remlin has returned to Abryryim, guided by fragmented visions in the night sky, ready to adventure, explore, and test the lessons of the stars against the living world of Men. He hopes one day to visit his mother again in the Feywild, but for now, he walks among mortals, charting the sky, seeking to understand what the constellations truly portend, and whether ought may be done about it.

Remlin Ast’eria

* Character Details:  
  * Alignment: Neutral Good  
    * Remlin believes fate exists, but also believes people deserve help navigating it.  
    * Remlin interprets destiny—and chooses mercy when possible.  
    * His visions come in fragments, dreams, star-charts, and symbolic omens.  
    * He intervenes to reduce suffering, not merely to preserve outcomes.  
    * He values guidance and knowledge over inevitability.  
* Faith:   
  * Oros al'Calhoon \[The Twin Soul\]   
    * Goddess of the moon  
    * Domains: Twilight/Light   
    * Focus: Change, transition, guidance, revelation  
* Personality Traits:   
  * Gentle until provoked, socially reserved until he feels needed/relevant, curious and studious until lost in what seem like visions...  
  * Str13/Dex15/Con16 \- lithe and hearty from surviving in a variety of wild terrains with various druid and wizard enclaves  
  * Int14/Wis17/Cha12 \- well-educated in arcane, history, and nature; well-practiced in the ways of following the stars to not be caught unawares and survive off the land; though these studies have taken a toll and Remlin is not always the most approachable  
* Ideals:  
  * Remlin has seen what he believes to be a portion of a Prophecy: **"Comfort…heavenly might guiding all…light."**    
    * He believes the stars will guide him to the rest of it.  
  * Guidance:  “If the path is dark, I aspire to light it.”  
    * Remlin believes foresight exists to help others make better choices, hopefully to prevent trapping them in inevitability.  
  * Balance: “The sky moves in cycles. So too, does life.”  
    * He respects fate and does believe in some inevitability, but rejects despairing in this.   
    * Remlin cares for these mortals as he believes they are chosen to guide him to the Truth within the Stars  
  * Hope:  “The stars endure. So too may we.”  
    * Even in decline or crisis, Remlin believes renewal is possible.  
    * What is foretold may still be mitigated?  
  * He adventures to:  
    * Test his interpretations of the cosmos and seek the Prophecy  
    * Discover whether the fate of Men can be influenced or guided  
    * Remlin is haunted yet motivated by a ruminating question: “If I could see a bit farther, might I change it?”  
* Bonds:  
  * The Living Sky:  
    * Remlin is bound to the stars as teachers, hoping to find they are not masters. He studies them for guidance and prays to Oros for wisdom.  
  * Places of Connection:  
    * Open skies, especially upon hilltops, mountaintops, at stone circles, or near ancient ruins  
    * Forests matter to the balance of Abryryim—but Remlin looks upward, not inward.  
  * Treasured Possession:  
    * Star Map: Constellation map etched onto the hide of an Owlbear, revised constantly. Includes notes, myths, and questions, a record of astrological learning.  
* Flaws:  
  * Uncertain Vision: Remlin’s omens are symbolic and incomplete. He may misinterpret signs or hesitate at critical moments.  On some level, Remlin fears seeing too far into what the stars may behold and that he may not be able to decipher it or react accordingly alone.
